Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the system log there are events like FOCUS TYPING SAVE SWITCH etc Each has an app and window title The window titles seem important they mention specific documents like Quarterly_Report.docx Document1.docx Report_Draft.pdf Budget_2024.xlsx Hmm the window title probably represents the document or task the user is working on

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So maybe each document is a case But wait sometimes the user switches between documents For example they start with Quarterly_Report.docx then switch to Document1.docx then to Chrome for email then to PDF then back to Excel then back to Document1.docx But in process mining a case is usually a single instance of a process If the user is working on multiple documents each document might be its own case But sometimes theyre working on related tasks across documents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But the problem says a case can represent a logical unit of user work such as editing a specific document handling a particular email or reviewing a certain PDF Ah so each document email or PDF could be its own case So Case for Document1.docx all events related to it Case for Quarterly_Report.docx its events Case for Email - Inbox but the email is about Annual Meeting so maybe thats a case Case for Report_Draft.pdf Case for Budget_2024.xlsx

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: But how to handle the email The window is Email - Inbox but the specific email is identified by the action Open Email about Annual Meeting So maybe the case ID for the email is Email - Annual Meeting Similarly for the PDF window is Report_Draft.pdf so case ID could be Review Report_Draft.pdf or just Report_Draft.pdf

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: SWITCH might not be an activity in the case In process mining we usually want the activities within a case So SWITCH might be ignored or considered as part of the case transition but since cases are separate maybe SWITCH events are not assigned to any case SWITCH events might be omitted from the event log as they dont belong to a specific cases activity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Let me think about activity naming The raw actions are FOCUS TYPING SAVE SWITCH CLICK SCROLL HIGHLIGHT CLOSE We need to map these to higher-level activities For a document case like Document1.docx FOCUS could be Open Document or Resume Editing TYPING Edit Content SAVE Save Document CLOSE Close Document

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Ah this is a key point I think I made a mistake earlier by considering Document1.docx as a case that is closed and then reopened but in reality the same document instance might be worked on in multiple sessions but its still one case

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So case identification group all events that share the same window title for documents or can be inferred as the same task

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: for Quarterly_Report the first event is FOCUS at 085950 then next event is FOCUS on Document1 at 090000 so Quarterly_Report is open but not focused Then at 090715 FOCUS back to Quarterly_Report So for Quarterly_Report the events are 085950 FOCUS Open Document 090715 FOCUS Resume Document

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Alternative map FOCUS to Access Document for all cases This seems consistent

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Timestamp Case ID Activity Name 2024-12-11T085950.000Z Quarterly_Report.docx Access Document 2024-12-11T090000.000Z Document1.docx Access Document 2024-12-11T090030.000Z Document1.docx Edit Document This should be analyst-friendly
